question: A standard dice has the numbers 1 to 6 on its faces such that opposite faces always add up to 7. Which number is opposite to 4?
explanation: On a standard dice, opposite faces are (1,6), (2,5), and (3,4). Therefore, the face opposite to 4 is 3.
learning_tip: Many placement questions use the standard dice unless stated otherwise.

question: In a standard dice, which of the following numbers is adjacent to 2?
explanation: The face opposite to 2 is 5. Therefore, 2 is adjacent to 1, 3, 4 and 6.
shortcut: A face is adjacent to every face except itself and its opposite.
formula: Adjacent Faces = Total Faces − Self − Opposite
learning_tip: Each face on a cube has exactly four adjacent faces.

question: A cube is placed on a table. How many faces of the cube are visible at the same time?
explanation: When a cube rests on a table, one face is at the bottom, leaving three faces visible simultaneously.
shortcut: Cube on a table → 3 visible faces.
learning_tip: Three faces meeting at one corner are always visible together.

question: If the top face of a standard dice is 5, which number is at the bottom?
explanation: In a standard dice, opposite faces are (1,6), (2,5), (3,4). Hence, the face opposite to 5 is 2.
learning_tip: Always verify whether the problem mentions a standard dice or a custom dice.

question: How many faces are adjacent to any one face of a cube?
explanation: Each face shares an edge with four other faces and is opposite to only one face.
shortcut: Every cube face has four neighbours.
learning_tip: A cube has 6 faces, each adjacent to 4 faces and opposite to 1 face.

question: How many faces meet at one corner (vertex) of a cube?
explanation: Exactly three faces intersect at every vertex of a cube.
learning_tip: Questions on cube corners are very common in placement exams.

question: A cube has how many edges?
explanation: A cube has 6 faces, 12 edges and 8 vertices.
learning_tip: Basic cube properties are frequently tested before advanced reasoning questions.

question: A dice is thrown once. What is the probability of getting an even number?
explanation: Even numbers on a dice are 2, 4 and 6. Thus, favourable outcomes = 3 and total outcomes = 6. Probability = 3/6 = 1/2.
learning_tip: Dice reasoning and probability are often combined in placement aptitude tests.

question: In a standard dice, which number is opposite to 1?
explanation: On a standard dice, opposite faces are (1,6), (2,5) and (3,4). Therefore, the face opposite to 1 is 6.
learning_tip: Standard dice questions usually depend on remembering opposite faces.

question: If 6 is on the top face of a standard dice, which of the following numbers cannot be on the front face?
explanation: The face opposite to 6 is 1. Opposite faces cannot be adjacent. Hence, 1 cannot appear on the front face.
shortcut: Opposite faces never touch each other.
learning_tip: Eliminate the opposite face first in cube and dice questions.

question: How many vertices does a cube have?
explanation: A cube has 6 faces, 12 edges and 8 vertices.
learning_tip: Cube properties are often asked before advanced reasoning questions.

question: A cube is painted on all six faces and then cut into 27 equal smaller cubes. How many small cubes will have exactly three painted faces?
explanation: Only the corner cubes have exactly three painted faces. A cube has 8 corners.
shortcut: Corner cubes always have three painted faces.
learning_tip: Painted cube problems are among the most common visual reasoning questions.

question: A cube is painted on all faces and cut into 64 equal smaller cubes. How many small cubes will have exactly two painted faces?
explanation: Exactly two painted faces occur on edge cubes excluding corners. Formula = 12 × (n−2). Here n=4. So 12 × 2 = 24.
learning_tip: Memorize the standard painted cube formulas for faster solving.

question: A cube is painted on all six faces and divided into 125 equal cubes. How many small cubes have exactly one painted face?
explanation: Exactly one painted face = 6 × (n−2)². Here n=5. Therefore 6 × 3² = 54.
learning_tip: Face-center cubes have exactly one painted face.

question: A cube is painted on all six faces and cut into 216 equal cubes. How many small cubes have no painted face?
explanation: No painted face = (n−2)³. Here n=6. So (6−2)³ = 64.
learning_tip: The inner cubes never receive any paint.

question: A cube is painted on all six faces and cut into 343 equal cubes. How many cubes will have at least one painted face?
explanation: n = 7. Total cubes = 7³ = 343. Inner cubes = (7−2)³ = 125. Cubes with at least one painted face = 343 − 125 = 218.
shortcut: At least one painted face = Total cubes − Inner cubes.
learning_tip: This is one of the most frequently asked painted cube questions in placement exams.

question: A cube is painted on all six faces and divided into 512 equal smaller cubes. How many small cubes will have exactly three painted faces?
explanation: Only the corner cubes have exactly three painted faces. Every cube has 8 corners.
shortcut: Corner cubes always have exactly 3 painted faces.
learning_tip: The number of corner cubes never changes, regardless of how many divisions are made.

question: A cube is painted on all six faces and cut into 729 equal cubes. How many cubes will have exactly two painted faces?
explanation: n = 9. Cubes with exactly two painted faces = 12 × (9−2) = 84.
learning_tip: Only edge cubes excluding corners have exactly two painted faces.

question: A cube is painted on all six faces and divided into 1000 equal cubes. How many cubes will have exactly one painted face?
explanation: n = 10. Cubes with exactly one painted face = 6 × (10−2)² = 6 × 64 = 384.
learning_tip: Face-center cubes always have exactly one painted face.

question: A cube is painted on all six faces and cut into 1331 equal cubes. How many cubes will have no painted face?
explanation: n = 11. Cubes with no painted face = (11−2)³ = 9³ = 729.
learning_tip: Only the inner cubes remain unpainted.

question: A cube is painted on all six faces and cut into 64 equal cubes. How many cubes will have at least one painted face?
explanation: n = 4. Total cubes = 64. Inner cubes = (4−2)³ = 8. Cubes with at least one painted face = 64 − 8 = 56.
shortcut: At least one painted face = Total cubes − Inner cubes.
learning_tip: Subtract only the completely unpainted inner cubes.

question: A standard dice is rolled twice. What is the probability that both rolls show even numbers?
explanation: Probability of an even number in one roll = 3/6 = 1/2. Therefore, (1/2) × (1/2) = 1/4.
learning_tip: Dice probability questions are frequently combined with visual reasoning.

question: A cube has how many diagonals joining opposite vertices?
explanation: A cube has exactly four body diagonals joining opposite vertices.
learning_tip: Do not confuse body diagonals with face diagonals.

question: A cube has 12 edges. If each edge is doubled in length, by what factor does its volume increase?
explanation: Volume of a cube is proportional to the cube of its edge length. Doubling the edge gives (2)³ = 8 times the original volume.
shortcut: Volume changes as the cube of the side length.
learning_tip: Remember: Area changes with the square of the scale factor, while volume changes with the cube.

question: A cube is painted on all six faces and cut into 8 × 8 × 8 = 512 equal smaller cubes. How many smaller cubes will have at least two painted faces?
explanation:
For n = 8:
• Cubes with exactly 3 painted faces (corners) = 8
• Cubes with exactly 2 painted faces (edge cubes excluding corners) = 12 × (8−2) = 72
Therefore, cubes with at least 2 painted faces = 8 + 72 = 80.
learning_tip:
Remember these painted cube formulas:
• 3 painted faces = 8
• 2 painted faces = 12(n−2)
• 1 painted face = 6(n−2)²
• No painted face = (n−2)³
These four formulas solve nearly every painted cube question asked in placement exams.
